 {"id":"bd-11mqo","title":"[SEC-5.3] Incident evidence bundle export and forensic replay UX","description":"## Background
Incident response needs deterministic, minimally sufficient artifacts that preserve privacy.

## Scope
- Export incident bundles containing relevant ledger slices, policy snapshots, and redacted traces.
- Provide replay utility to reconstruct timeline and decisions.
- Define redaction and retention controls.

## Deliverables
- Bundle format spec and export CLI/RPC path.
- Replay command docs and validation checks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bundle generation is deterministic for same incident scope.
- [ ] Sensitive data is redacted per policy.
- [ ] Replay can reproduce key enforcement events for triage.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)

Will implement: (1) incident evidence bundle format with deterministic generation, (2) ledger slice + policy snapshot + redacted trace export, (3) replay utility for decision timeline reconstruction, (4) redaction/retention controls integrated with secret broker policy.","created_at":"2026-02-14T11:06:50Z"},{"id":3911,"issue_id":"bd-11mqo","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEC-5.3 implementation complete. Added:

**Types** (src/extensions.rs):
- `IncidentBundleFilter`: time-window, extension-id, alert category, min severity filtering
- `IncidentBundleRedactionPolicy`: 6-field privacy control (params_hash, context_hash, args_shape_hash, command_hash, name_hash, remediation)
- `IncidentEvidenceBundle`: self-contained bundle with all 6 artifact types + replay + summary + integrity hash
- `IncidentBundleSummary`: 10-field quick triage (counts, peak risk, deny count, chain integrity)
- `IncidentBundleVerificationReport`: 6-field integrity report

**Functions** (src/extensions.rs):
- `build_incident_evidence_bundle()`: deterministic bundle construction with filtering + redaction
- `compute_incident_bundle_hash()`: SHA-256 integrity seal over all content sections
- `verify_incident_evidence_bundle()`: schema + hash + chain + summary cross-checks
- `export_incident_bundle()` on ExtensionManager: convenience method collecting all artifacts

**Tests** (tests/incident_evidence_bundle.rs): 30 integration tests covering:
- Construction, deterministic generation, time/extension/category/severity filtering
- Default + custom redaction, integrity verification, replay, JSON roundtrip, chain integrity

All 30 tests pass. Add deterministic automated tests for message queue semantics.

# Scope
- Queue ordering
- Delivery boundaries
- one-at-a-time vs all modes
- abort + restore behavior
- dequeue behavior

+{"id":"bd-121mf","title":"Session store integrity must reject dangling parent references","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found that SessionStoreV2::validate_integrity() records parent links and checks for cycles/duplicates, but it does not reject frames whose parent_entry_id points to a missing entry. That lets corrupted stores pass integrity validation and can cause migration_status() to report Migrated while read_active_path() silently truncates history. Fix validate_integrity() to fail closed on missing parent references and add focused regression coverage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-07T05:42:43.747243631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T05:43:00.107599616Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-123","title":"Spec: PiJS runtime contract + event loop semantics","description":"# Goal
Define the **authoritative PiJS runtime contract** for running JS/TS extensions **without Node/Bun**, with a **deterministic, testable event loop** and an explicit, capability-gated hostcall surface.

This spec is the reference for:
- bd-8mm (scheduler), bd-2ke (QuickJS promise/job bridge), bd-1f5 (JS runtime), bd-320/bd-xgo (pipeline), and all test harness work.

# Assumptions / Constraints
- **Assume QuickJS has no WebAssembly**: any JS bundle expecting `globalThis.WebAssembly` must use the PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry) or Tier A WASM components.
- No ambient OS APIs: all side effects must flow through the connector dispatcher (bd-h04) and therefore capability checks + structured logs.

# Definitions (terms)
- **Microtasks**: the QuickJS job queue (Promise reactions, queueMicrotask).
- **Macrotasks**: host-driven tasks (timers, inbound extension events, hostcall completions).
- **Tick**: one deterministic scheduling step that runs at most one macrotask plus a full microtask drain.
- **Hostcall**: a side-effecting request from JS to the host, enforced by capability policy, represented in protocol terms as `host_call`/`host_result` (bd-37z).

# 1) Module / Artifact Loader Contract
## 1.1 Artifact inputs
PiJS executes artifacts produced by extc (bd-xgo) from pinned sources (sample set in `docs/extension-sample.json`).

Extc output must be:
- deterministic (byte-for-byte stable under identical inputs)
- ESM-resolvable inside PiJS
- sourcemap-correct (runtime errors map to original TS/JS)

## 1.2 Allowed specifiers and resolution
The PiJS module resolver MUST:
- resolve relative specifiers (`./` and `../`) within the artifact
- resolve internal Pi-provided modules under a reserved namespace (recommended: `pi:*`)
- forbid network imports (`http:`/`https:`) and other ambient loaders

Recommended canonicalization performed by extc (bd-2ki): rewrite Node builtins to `pi:node/*` and inject any required polyfills deterministically.

## 1.3 Initialization contract
- The host loads the artifact entry module.
- The entry module must export a default function that receives the host-provided `pi` object (ExtensionAPI surface).
- Any thrown error during load/initialization is mapped to an extension error with sourcemapped location and emitted as structured log events.

# 2) The `pi` API Contract (JS-facing)
The `pi` object provided to extensions is the single ambient authority. It MUST be capability-gated internally.

## 2.1 Registration surface (protocol-facing)
At minimum (shape may follow the legacy API):
- `pi.registerTool(spec)`
- `pi.registerSlashCommand(spec)`
- `pi.on(event_name, handler)` for lifecycle/tool-call hooks
- provider registration APIs for provider extensions (if applicable in the sample)

Semantics:
- Registration MUST be idempotent per (extension_id, name).
- Invalid specs must fail fast with actionable errors.
- Registration affects what the host advertises/dispatches for that extension.

## 2.2 Connector surface (hostcall-facing)
At minimum:
- `pi.exec(cmd, args, options) -> Promise<{ stdout, stderr, exitCode }>`
- `pi.http(request) -> Promise<response>`
- `pi.session.*` accessors/mutations as defined by protocol
- `pi.ui.*` primitives (select/input/confirm/editor) that can be denied in non-interactive mode
- `pi.log(level, event, data)` for extension-authored logs

Rules:
- Every connector method maps to a `host_call` with a `call_id`, capability, method, params, timeout/cancel metadata.
- Every connector method MUST emit structured audit logs through bd-h04.
- Errors MUST map onto the hostcall error taxonomy (Denied/Timeout/IO/InvalidRequest/Internal).

## 2.3 Cancellation + timeouts
- Any async connector call MAY accept an AbortSignal; cancellation must map to hostcall cancel_token semantics.
- Timeouts must be enforced in the dispatcher; JS receives a deterministic Timeout error.

# 3) PiJS Event Loop: Formal State Machine
## 3.1 State
Define the runtime state as:

- `seq: u64` monotone counter (total-order tie-breaker)
- `Q_micro`: the QuickJS job queue (internal to engine; host can drain)
- `Q_macro`: FIFO queue of macrotasks, each tagged with an enqueue `seq`
- `Q_timer`: min-heap of timers keyed by `(deadline_ms, seq)`
- `Q_host`: conceptual source of hostcall completions (host must enqueue into `Q_macro` deterministically)
- `clock`: monotonic time source (injectable for tests)

Each macrotask is one of:
- `TimerFired(timer_id)`
- `HostcallComplete(call_id, outcome)`
- `InboundEvent(event_id, payload)` (tool_call, slash_command, lifecycle hook, ui response, etc.)

## 3.2 The `tick()` algorithm
`tick(state)` must be deterministic given the current state and the set of newly-arrived host completions.

Algorithm (normative):
1) **Ingest host completions**: any completed hostcalls since last tick are enqueued into `Q_macro` with a deterministic order key.
   - Recommended: assign each completion an enqueue `seq` in arrival order using the monotone counter.
2) **Move due timers**: while `Q_timer.min.deadline_ms <= clock.now_ms`, pop timers and enqueue `TimerFired` into `Q_macro` (preserving `(deadline_ms, seq)` order).
3) **Run one macrotask**:
   - If `Q_macro` non-empty: pop the lowest `seq` macrotask and execute it.
   - Else: idle (no-op).
4) **Drain microtasks to fixpoint**: repeatedly drain the QuickJS job queue until it is empty.
5) Return updated state.

## 3.3 Invariants (must hold)
- **I1 (single macrotask):** at most one macrotask executes per tick.
- **I2 (microtask fixpoint):** after any macrotask, microtasks are drained until empty.
- **I3 (stable timers):** timers with equal deadlines fire in increasing `seq` order.
- **I4 (no reentrancy):** hostcall completions do not synchronously re-enter JS; they enqueue macrotasks.
- **I5 (total order):** all externally observable scheduling is ordered by `seq` (deterministic tie-break).

## 3.4 Timers contract
- `setTimeout(fn, ms)` enqueues a timer with `(deadline_ms = clock.now_ms + ms, seq = next_seq())`.
- `clearTimeout(id)` removes it if pending.
- `setInterval` is optional unless required by the pinned sample; if implemented, it must be specified in terms of repeated `setTimeout` with stable ordering.

## 3.5 Hostcall completion contract
- Each hostcall has a stable `call_id` and (recommended) an issuance `seq`.
- Completion enqueuing must be deterministic:
  - In production: order by completion arrival, but *stabilize* with the monotone seq.
  - In tests: completion order can be controlled by recorded fixtures / deterministic runtime.

# 4) Determinism Contract (formal-ish)
## 4.1 What we promise
Given:
- identical artifact bytes + shim versions
- identical initial state
- identical sequence of inbound events (tool calls, lifecycle events, UI responses)
- identical sequence of hostcall results (including their enqueue order)
- identical clock behavior (or a deterministic clock)

Then:
- the sequence of executed macrotasks and the resulting observable outputs (tool results, logs, UI prompts) are identical.

## 4.2 Proof sketch (why)
- The scheduler is a pure function of `(state, arrivals)` with a total-order tie-breaker `seq`.
- Timer ordering is deterministic via `(deadline_ms, seq)`.
- Hostcall completion ordering is deterministic by construction (completion enqueue `seq`).
- Microtask draining to a fixpoint ensures no hidden interleavings.
- Therefore, by induction over ticks, the entire execution trace is deterministic under fixed inputs.

# 5) Observability / Trace Contract
- Every tick and every enqueue/dequeue event MAY be logged (debug-level) under `pi.ext.log.v1` with `trace_id`/`span_id` and correlation ids.
- Deterministic test runs must be able to compare traces for equality after normalization.

# Non-goals
- Full Node/Bun parity.
- Ambient OS access.
- Undefined behavior around timer ordering or microtask starvation.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:21:41.522544650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:49.783437989Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:18:26.610788213Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3866,"issue_id":"bd-123","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting bd-123 (P0): will author the PiJS runtime contract + deterministic event loop semantics in EXTENSIONS.md (authoritative), including:
- `pi.*` global surface + `registerExtension(...)` contract
- ESM loader contract (what extc outputs, resolution, forbidden APIs)
- Event loop state machine (queues + transitions), ordering invariants, and determinism proof sketch
- Hostcall promise bridge semantics (resolve/reject ordering, timeout/cancel)
- Debug hooks + structured logging expectations + user-facing diagnostics

I’ll keep protocol/schema references consistent with docs/schema/extension_protocol.json and current scaffolding in src/extensions_js.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:16:54Z"},{"id":3867,"issue_id":"bd-123","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Stopping work on bd-123 per user request (2026-02-03): do NOT want me touching extensions-related work. Un-claiming so other agents can proceed.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:23:52Z"},{"id":3868,"issue_id":"bd-123","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Authored PiJS runtime contract in EXTENSIONS.md §1A.4 (Normative): loader/specifier rules, pi API + hostcall mapping, deterministic tick() (macrotask + microtask drain), timer/hostcall ordering invariants, and trace/determinism contract. This should unblock bd-8mm/bd-2ke/bd-2ki.","created_at":"2026-02-03T20:18:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1247","title":"Unit tests: extension_popularity.rs — URL parsing, response deserialization, slug guesses","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteFinch","created_at":"2026-02-06T18:23:01.915488853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:26:20.829702040Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:26:20.829671162Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1277x","title":"[AUTH-TEST] Comprehensive tests for authentication parity features","description":"## Overview

Comprehensive test coverage for all AUTH track features with structured JSONL logging for debugging and CI artifact retention.

## Test Infrastructure

### JSONL Logging Helper
All tests use the log_test_event pattern for structured debugging:
```rust
fn log_test_event(test_name: &str, event: &str, data: &serde_json::Value) {
    let entry = serde_json::json\!({
        "schema": "pi.test.auth_event.v1",
        "test": test_name,
        "event": event,
        "timestamp_ms": std::time::SystemTime::now()
            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap().as_millis(),
        "data": data,
    });
    eprintln\!("JSONL: {}", serde_json::to_string(&entry).unwrap());
}
```

Events captured per test:
- `test_start` — test name, scenario description
- `setup_complete` — fixture state (e.g., auth.json contents, mock server ready)
- `action` — what was triggered (e.g., OAuth URL generated, token exchange attempted)
- `assertion` — expected vs actual values
- `test_end` — pass/fail, duration_ms

---

## Unit Tests (src/auth.rs tests module)

### OpenAI OAuth/APIKey Flow
1. `test_openai_oauth_url_generation` — If OAuth supported: correct auth URL with PKCE challenge, scopes, redirect_uri. If API key mode: prompt for key stores correctly.
   - JSONL: `{"event":"url_generated","data":{"url":"...","has_pkce":true,"scopes":["openid"]}}`
2. `test_openai_token_exchange` — VCR-backed exchange: authorization_code -> access_token + refresh_token. Verify auth.json updated.
   - JSONL: `{"event":"token_exchanged","data":{"provider":"openai","has_refresh":true,"expires_in":3600}}`

### Google OAuth Flow
3. `test_google_oauth_url_generation` — Correct URL with access_type=offline, prompt=consent, generative-language scope
   - JSONL: `{"event":"url_generated","data":{"url":"...","access_type":"offline","prompt":"consent"}}`
4. `test_google_token_exchange` — VCR-backed exchange with refresh_token (Google returns refresh only on first consent)
   - JSONL: `{"event":"token_exchanged","data":{"provider":"google","has_refresh":true}}`

### Refresh Failure Recovery
5. `test_refresh_failure_produces_recovery_action` — RefreshFailure struct contains provider name, error type (auth vs network), and recovery action text ("/login {provider}")
   - JSONL: `{"event":"refresh_failed","data":{"provider":"anthropic","error_type":"invalid_grant","recovery":"/login anthropic"}}`
6. `test_refresh_failure_network_vs_auth_different_messages` — Network errors show "check connection", auth errors show "/login"
   - JSONL: `{"event":"error_classified","data":{"error_type":"network","message":"Check your network connection"}}`

### Provider Listing
7. `test_provider_listing_shows_all_providers` — List includes built-in (anthropic, openai, google) + any extension providers
8. `test_provider_listing_shows_expiry` — OAuth tokens show human-readable expiry ("expires in 23 hours")
9. `test_provider_listing_no_credentials` — Provider with no stored credentials shows "Not authenticated"

---

## TUI State Tests (tests/tui_state.rs)

10. `tui_login_no_args_shows_provider_table` — /login with no args renders table with all providers and their auth status
    - JSONL: `{"event":"provider_table_rendered","data":{"providers":["anthropic","openai","google"],"authenticated":["anthropic"]}}`
11. `tui_login_openai_starts_auth_flow` — /login openai triggers the appropriate auth flow (OAuth or API key prompt)
    - JSONL: `{"event":"auth_flow_started","data":{"provider":"openai","flow_type":"oauth|apikey"}}`
12. `tui_refresh_failure_shows_recovery_message` — RefreshFailure results in visible system message with actionable /login suggestion
    - JSONL: `{"event":"system_message_shown","data":{"message_contains":"/login anthropic"}}`
13. `tui_login_unknown_provider_shows_error` — /login unknown-provider shows clear error listing valid providers
14. `tui_login_gemini_aliases_to_google` — /login gemini internally routes to Google OAuth flow

---

## E2E Tests (tests/auth_oauth_refresh_vcr.rs)

### E2E Script 1: Full OAuth Dance
```bash
# Script: test_full_oauth_dance.sh
# Verifies: complete OAuth flow from /login to stored credentials
#
# 1. Start mock OAuth server (serves authorize + token endpoints)
# 2. Launch pi with --test-mode
# 3. Send /login openai
# 4. Verify auth URL opened (mock captures redirect)
# 5. Simulate callback with authorization_code
# 6. Verify auth.json contains access_token + refresh_token
# 7. Verify subsequent API call uses access_token in Authorization header
#
# JSONL events:
# {"event":"mock_server_started","data":{"port":...}}
# {"event":"auth_url_captured","data":{"url":"...","provider":"openai"}}
# {"event":"callback_simulated","data":{"code":"test_auth_code"}}
# {"event":"token_stored","data":{"provider":"openai","auth_json_path":"..."}}
# {"event":"api_call_verified","data":{"auth_header":"Bearer ..."}}
```

### E2E Script 2: Token Refresh During Agent Run
```bash
# Script: test_refresh_during_agent_run.sh
# Verifies: transparent token refresh mid-conversation
#
# 1. Setup auth.json with token expiring in 5 minutes
# 2. Start mock provider that returns 401 on first call, 200 after refresh
# 3. Launch pi with prompt "hello"
# 4. Verify refresh triggered (mock sees token refresh request)
# 5. Verify agent completes successfully with new token
# 6. Verify auth.json updated with new expiry
#
# JSONL events:
# {"event":"token_near_expiry","data":{"expires_in_seconds":300}}
# {"event":"refresh_triggered","data":{"provider":"anthropic"}}
# {"event":"refresh_completed","data":{"new_expires_in":3600}}
# {"event":"agent_completed","data":{"response_received":true}}
```

### E2E Script 3: Refresh Failure Recovery Path
```bash
# Script: test_refresh_failure_recovery.sh
# Verifies: graceful handling when refresh fails
#
# 1. Setup auth.json with expired token
# 2. Start mock that returns 401 on refresh (invalid_grant)
# 3. Launch pi with prompt
# 4. Verify error message shown to user
# 5. Verify /login suggestion is displayed
# 6. Verify agent enters Idle state (no crash, no hang)
#
# JSONL events:
# {"event":"refresh_attempted","data":{"provider":"anthropic"}}
# {"event":"refresh_failed","data":{"error":"invalid_grant","http_status":401}}
# {"event":"recovery_shown","data":{"message":"/login anthropic"}}
# {"event":"agent_state","data":{"state":"idle"}}
```

### E2E Script 4: Concurrent Refresh Race Condition
```bash
# Script: test_concurrent_refresh.sh
# Verifies: file lock prevents auth.json corruption
#
# 1. Setup auth.json with token expiring in 1 minute
# 2. Launch two pi processes simultaneously (RPC + interactive)
# 3. Both trigger refresh at the same time
# 4. Verify file lock coordinates access
# 5. Verify auth.json is valid JSON after both complete
# 6. Verify only one refresh request was made to provider
#
# JSONL events:
# {"event":"lock_acquired","data":{"process":"A","attempt":1}}
# {"event":"lock_waiting","data":{"process":"B","attempt":1}}
# {"event":"refresh_completed","data":{"process":"A"}}
# {"event":"lock_acquired","data":{"process":"B","attempt":2}}
# {"event":"token_fresh","data":{"process":"B","skipped_refresh":true}}
```

---

## Files
- src/auth.rs (unit tests in #[cfg(test)] module)
- tests/tui_state.rs (TUI state tests — add to existing)
- tests/auth_oauth_refresh_vcr.rs (E2E tests — extend existing file)
- tests/fixtures/vcr/oauth_*.json (VCR cassettes for token exchange)

## Dependencies
- Depends on AUTH-1 through AUTH-5

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 14+ test cases covering all AUTH features
- [ ] JSONL logging in all tests via log_test_event helper with pi.test.auth_event.v1 schema
- [ ] 4 E2E scripts with detailed JSONL event logging
- [ ] VCR cassettes for token exchange (no real network calls in CI)
- [ ] Unit tests for URL generation, token exchange, refresh failure, provider listing
- [ ] TUI state tests for /login UI behavior
- [ ] All tests pass deterministically
- [ ] Clippy clean","notes":"Completed AUTH-TEST coverage pass. Added explicit auth test-event helper (schema pi.test.auth_event.v1) in src/auth.rs tests and added/validated named cases: test_openai_oauth_url_generation, test_openai_token_exchange, test_google_oauth_url_generation, test_google_token_exchange, test_refresh_failure_produces_recovery_action, test_refresh_failure_network_vs_auth_different_messages, plus provider-listing status assertions via credential_status tests. Added explicit TUI auth tests in tests/tui_state.rs: tui_login_no_args_shows_provider_table, tui_login_openai_starts_auth_flow, tui_refresh_failure_shows_recovery_message, tui_login_unknown_provider_shows_error, tui_login_gemini_aliases_to_google, all emitting pi.test.auth_event.v1 JSONL. Updated tests/auth_oauth_refresh_vcr.rs log_refresh_event to emit pi.test.auth_event.v1 JSONL for refresh scenarios. Validation: targeted auth/tui/e2e tests pass (including auth_oauth_refresh_success_vcr); cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings pass.
